session_summary:
TEL-002 completed: SubagentPhaseChanged events now fire at tool call boundaries.

## What was done

### Created: packages/opencode/src/coordination/coord-events.ts
- Defined `SubagentPhaseChanged` Bus event with type `coord.subagent.phase`
- Schema: session_id, tool_call_id, tool_name, phase (started/completed/failed), description, changed_at
- Follows existing BusEvent.define pattern from bus/bus-event.ts

### Created: packages/opencode/src/tool/execution.ts
- Three helper functions: publishStarted, publishCompleted, publishFailed
- Each wraps bus.publish(CoordEvents.SubagentPhaseChanged, {...}) with appropriate phase value
- Follows Effect pattern — returns Effect.Effect<void>

### Modified: packages/opencode/src/session/tools.ts
- Added Bus.Service dependency (yield* Bus.Service)
- Added Execution.publishStarted() before item.execute() for built-in tools
- Added Effect.catch() wrapper with Execution.publishFailed() on error for built-in tools
- Added Execution.publishCompleted() after successful built-in tool execution
- Same pattern applied to MCP tool execution path
- Added required imports (Bus, Execution)

## Verification
- Typecheck passes with 0 new errors (only 2 pre-existing test errors)
- All existing functionality preserved
next_steps: TEL-001 (Bus events in task.ts) needs to be committed — the archived version has the changes but they're not in the source tree. The coord-events.ts created here also serves as the foundation for TEL-001's TaskStatusChanged event if needed.
